golden_responses.golden_text_response:
The draft email indicates that Anthony Porter failed to enforce the work stop directive dated October 5, 2025.  However, the work stop directive dated October 5, 2025 was only addressed to SteelTree Solutions and not to Anthony Porter.  There is no record in the personnel file that Mr. Porter received the work stop directive.  There is no record in the communications map that Mr. Porter received the work stop directive.  Making a claim to OSHA that is not supported by the evidence could damage SBP’s credibility with OSHA.  

Additionally, Mr. Porter’s failure to enforce the work stop directive dated October 5, 2025 is not mentioned in his November 3, 2025 Performance Review or in his November 4, 2025 Notice of Termination.  SBP not mentioning Anthony Porter’s failure to enforce the work stop directive dated October 5, 2025 in his November 3, 2025 Performance Review or his November 4, 2025 Notice of Termination but later mentioning it in the response to Anthony Porter’s retaliation complaint with OSHA appears to be a post-hoc justification or pretext for his termination, as it was not listed as a factor considered at the time of termination.  SBP making a claim to OSHA that appears to be admission of a post hoc justification for firing could damage SBP’s credibility with OSHA.  

The draft email is not sufficient and may harm SBP’s defense against Anthony Porter’s retaliation claim.
